stance_by_source.summary: Chart marks NIO as a long-term-discount buy: enter into the lower demand zone and target a recovery up toward 8.20, then 11+.
Graded the setup: buy at 4.55, targets 8.13 / 11 / 16.18 / 19 — but only a partial 5/10 ('HTF discount + automotive').
Imported trade marked already 'In': entry $4.55, first target $8.20, wrong below $2.70.

conflicts: No directional disagreement — all sources are bullish on the same $4.55 entry. Caveat: the live price (5.05) has already risen above that ideal entry, so the buy zone hasn't filled at current levels — chasing here worsens the risk:reward.
Zero's own grade rates this only a partial setup (5/10), and the chart shows a lot of unfilled liquidity above plus a deep macro-discount floor below — so conviction is capped despite the agreement.
plain_english: Every source agrees NIO is a long-term bargain-buy, not a quick trade. The plan is to buy near $4.55 — a price the chart marks as a strong buying zone — and ride a recovery toward $8.20 first, then potentially $11 and higher; the idea is wrong if price falls under about $2.70. Right now the price is $5.05, already above that $4.55 buy zone, so don't chase it. Wait for a dip back into the ~$4.55 demand area before entering.
